proposed_relations.description: 22,000 daily riders on the Bx41 route

evidence_refs.source_quote: Select Bus Service (SBS) is coming to Webster Avenue through a partnership between the New York City Department of Transportation (DOT) and MTA New York City Transit (NYCT). See the photo below to learn about SBS improvements, which will make bus travel faster and more reliable for the 22,000 daily riders on the Bx41 route.

decisions.rationale: The block explicitly states 22,000 daily riders on the Bx41 route, which corresponds to the existing Bx41 Limited service.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Select Bus Service (SBS) is coming to Webster Avenue through a partnership between the New York City Department of Transportation (DOT) and MTA New York City Transit (NYCT). See the photo below to learn about SBS improvements, which will make bus travel faster and more reliable for the 22,000 daily riders on the Bx41 route.

proposed_relations.description: 158 businesses surveyed by the Webster Avenue SBS project team

evidence_refs.source_quote: In August, the project team surveyed 158 businesses in the vicinity of the Bx41 Limited bus stops.

decisions.rationale: The project team conducted this survey as part of the Webster Avenue SBS project; the block names the project team directly.

evidence_refs.source_quote: In August, the project team surveyed 158 businesses in the vicinity of the Bx41 Limited bus stops.

decisions.rationale: This metric describes general SBS benefits from other implemented routes (Bx12, M15, M34/34A, S79), not specific to any named Webster Avenue subject. Per rule, only relate when cited text names a specific subject.

evidence_refs.source_quote: ▪ 15% to 20% faster trips ▪ 33% reduction in time spent at bus stops ▪ 10% reduction in all other travel time ▪ Over 95% customer satisfaction ▪ 10% ridership increase on the entire route, including SBS and local

decisions.rationale: General SBS benefit from other implemented routes; does not name a specific Webster Avenue subject.

proposed_relations.description: Over 85% of surveyed businesses rely on Webster Avenue curb space for deliveries

evidence_refs.source_quote: In August, the project team surveyed 158 businesses in the vicinity of the Bx41 Limited bus stops. The survey found that over 85% of businesses rely on Webster Avenue curb space for their deliveries.

decisions.rationale: The survey was conducted by the project team as part of the Webster Avenue SBS project; the metric is attributed to the project's business outreach effort.

evidence_refs.source_quote: In August, the project team surveyed 158 businesses in the vicinity of the Bx41 Limited bus stops. The survey found that over 85% of businesses rely on Webster Avenue curb space for their deliveries.

proposed_relations.description: Bx41 SBS fare equals local bus fare

evidence_refs.source_quote: ▪ Bx41 SBS replaces the Bx41 Limited ▪ Bx41 local route remains the same ▪ SBS fare = local bus fare

decisions.rationale: The block explicitly discusses Bx41 SBS features; the SBS fare claim is a characteristic of the Bx41 SBS route.

evidence_refs.source_quote: ▪ Bx41 SBS replaces the Bx41 Limited ▪ Bx41 local route remains the same ▪ SBS fare = local bus fare

proposed_relations.description: Customer parking and double parking identified as concerns in the Webster Avenue SBS business survey

evidence_refs.source_quote: Additionally, customer parking and double parking were identified as concerns.

decisions.rationale: The claim comes from the business survey conducted by the project team for the Webster Avenue SBS project.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Additionally, customer parking and double parking were identified as concerns.

proposed_relations.description: Survey information will guide DOT in improving curb regulations along the Webster Avenue corridor

evidence_refs.source_quote: This information will guide DOT in improving curb regulations in order to create better delivery space for businesses along the corridor and discourage double-parking.

decisions.rationale: The claim is about the project's use of survey findings to improve curb regulations as part of the Webster Avenue SBS effort.

evidence_refs.source_quote: This information will guide DOT in improving curb regulations in order to create better delivery space for businesses along the corridor and discourage double-parking.
